segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “It is inevitable that stumbling blocks will come, but woe to the one through whom they come!

2.text: It would be better for him to have a millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea than to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
2.speaker: Jesus

3.text: Watch yourselves. If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
3.speaker: Jesus

4.text: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to say, 'I repent,' you must forgive him.”
4.speaker: Jesus

verses.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “If you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and planted in the sea,' and it will obey you.

7.text: Which of you whose servant comes in from plowing or shepherding in the field will say to him, 'Come at once and sit down to eat'?
7.speaker: Jesus

8.text: Instead, won't he tell him, 'Prepare my meal and dress yourself to serve me while I eat and drink, and afterward you may eat and drink'?
8.speaker: Jesus

9.text: Does he thank the servant because he did what he was told?
9.speaker: Jesus

10.text: So you also, when you have done everything commanded of you, should say, 'We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.'”
10.speaker: Jesus

verses.11: While Jesus was on His way to Jerusalem, He was passing between Samaria and Galilee.
verses.12: As He entered one of the villages, He was met by ten lepers. They stood at a distance
verses.13: and raised their voices, shouting, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: When Jesus saw them, He said,

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: And as they were on their way, they were cleansed.

verses.15: When one of them saw that he was healed, he came back, praising God in a loud voice.
verses.16: He fell facedown at Jesus' feet in thanksgiving to Him—and he was a Samaritan.

18.text: Was no one found except this foreigner to return and give glory to God?”
18.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “Rise and go; your faith has made you well!”

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: When asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, Jesus replied,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “The kingdom of God will not come with observable signs.

21.text: Nor will people say, 'Look, here it is,' or 'There it is.' For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”
21.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: Then He said to the disciples,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.

23.text: People will tell you, 'Look, there He is!' or 'Look, here He is!' Do not go out or chase after them.
23.speaker: Jesus

24.text: For just as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so will be the Son of Man in His day.
24.speaker: Jesus

25.text: But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
25.speaker: Jesus

26.text: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man:
26.speaker: Jesus

27.text: People were eating and drinking, marrying and being given in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
27.speaker: Jesus

28.text: It was the same in the days of Lot: People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.
28.speaker: Jesus

29.text: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
29.speaker: Jesus

30.text: It will be just like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
30.speaker: Jesus

31.text: On that day, let no one on the housetop come down to retrieve his possessions. Likewise, let no one in the field return for anything he has left behind.
31.speaker: Jesus

33.text: Whoever tries to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.
33.speaker: Jesus

34.text: I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed: one will be taken and the other left.
34.speaker: Jesus

35.text: Two women will be grinding grain together: one will be taken and the other left.”
35.speaker: Jesus

36.text: Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other left.
36.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.”

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
